title: Core UI: floating button bar with mode-aware hue and mode-specific buttons
description:
## Problem

The floating UI currently has two independent pieces: the command menu (bottom-right) and the workshop panel (also bottom-right, offset). There is no unified system for mode-aware floating actions. The command button color is hardcoded to slate regardless of the active mode, and mode-specific tools (like workshop) mount themselves independently rather than composing into a shared toolbar surface.

## Solution

Formalize a Core UI button bar — a fixed floating bar in the bottom-right that acts as the single surface for all floating actions. The bar always shows the command button (rightmost, largest). Mode-specific buttons appear to its left at a smaller size. All buttons use the TriggerButton component and their hue follows the active mode collar color via the existing CSS custom property theming (--trigger-bg, --trigger-bg-hover, --trigger-text, --trigger-border).

The command menu remains present across all modes. Its dropdown options may change depending on the active mode.

The workshop button is the first mode-specific button. It appears only in develop mode (inspect), renders next to the command button at a smaller TriggerButton size, and opens a menu/overlay with the Create Prototype feature. The workshop overlay should render without emoji icons on the left of items.

## Scope

In scope:
- Core UI button bar container (fixed, bottom-right, arranges buttons horizontally)
- TriggerButton size variant for smaller mode-specific buttons
- Mode-aware hue: TriggerButton CSS custom properties driven by the active mode collar color
- Workshop button as the first mode-specific button (develop/inspect mode only)
- Workshop menu items render without emoji icons
- Create Prototype remains the workshop feature, accessible from the workshop button

Out of scope:
- Other mode-specific buttons (future work per mode)
- Replacing the ModeSwitch component (stays as-is, bottom-center)
- ToolbarShell (already exists but is a separate concern)
- Changes to the Create Prototype form/overlay itself (only its trigger location changes)

title: Add smaller size variant to TriggerButton (e.g. icon-lg or icon-xl) for mode-specific buttons
Create CoreUIBar container component that arranges command + mode buttons horizontally (fixed bottom-right)
Wire mode collar colors to TriggerButton CSS custom properties so hue follows active mode
Move CommandMenu trigger into CoreUIBar and make its menu options mode-aware
Create WorkshopButton as the first mode-specific button (visible only in inspect/develop mode)
Integrate WorkshopPanel menu and Create Prototype overlay into the WorkshopButton (without emoji icons)
Remove standalone workshop mount in favor of CoreUIBar-driven rendering
Verify Core UI bar works across all modes with correct hue transitions and button visibility
